Caption: Geometric design process of a complex re-entrant honeycomb. (a) Coordinates of discretized internal nodes. (b) Geometry of a multi-node fundamental domain. (c) Configuration of the complex re-entrant lattice, showing its fundamental domain (yellow) and unit cell (green).

Loading analysis of a typical multi-node, S-shaped strut within a fundamental domain. (a) Load applied along the Y-direction. (b) Discrete model showing the boundary conditions of the S-shaped strut and the displacements of the beam element in the local coordinate system.
Optimization constraints within the fundamental domain and detailed geometric characteristics of the cell walls.

Comparison of different re-entrant structure unit cells and their corresponding equivalent Poisson's ratios: (a) optimized multi-node re-entrant metastructure, (b) vertical-strut combined re-entrant honeycomb structure proposed in [88], and (c) conventional re-entrant hexagonal honeycomb structure.
Finite element model and boundary conditions of a typical metastructure.

Numerical results showing the variation of Poisson’s ratio with strain for the optimized multi-node re-entrant metastructure: (a) under compressive loading, (b) under tensile loading, (c) comparison with conventional configurations under compressive loading, and (d) comparison with conventional configurations under tensile loading.

Optimized metastructures with varying x B and y B. (a) Variation of the optimized Poisson’s ratio with respect to x B and y B. (b) Representative optimized configurations corresponding to different values of x B and y B.
Optimization of the 3D multi-node re-entrant honeycomb. (a) Geometry of the one-eighth unit cell. (b) Optimized unit cell configuration. (c) Periodic lattice structure of the optimized metastructure.
Numerical results of Poisson’s ratio variation with strain for the optimized 3D multi-node re-entrant metastructure. (a) Under compressive loading. (b) Under tensile loading.
Comparison of the stress-strain curve and Poisson's ratio variation predicted by the Updated Lagrangian method and finite element analysis.

Comparison between predicted and FEA results of the optimized metastructure under large deformation for a multi-node re-entrant design with n = 8. The lower-left inset illustrates the unit cell of the optimized configuration.

Multi-node re-entrant metastructure with optimized Poisson’s ratio under large deformation for n = 13. (a) Unit cell of the optimized configuration. (b) Comparison between predicted and numerical results of the optimized metastructure under large deformation. (c) Variation of Poisson’s ratio with strain under compression.

Comparison of optimized configurations under large deformation. (a) Unit cell of the optimized configuration with n = 18. (b) Comparison of numerical results for εₘₐₓ among configurations with n = 8, 13, and 18, and the conventional configurations.
